title: I/O Contracts, Files, Serialization, Time, and Command-Line Programs · Go Build Room
summary: A command must stream records, validate JSON, write atomically, handle time and cancellation, and produce useful exit evidence. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a deterministic import and reporting command while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compose io.Reader and io.Writer boundaries, buffering, limited reads, copying, and streaming without assuming one read fills a buffer.
Open files with explicit ownership and permissions, handle paths safely, and design temp-write, sync, rename, and cleanup evidence.
Encode and decode JSON with field visibility, tags, unknown-field policy, numbers, nulls, optional values, and streaming limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Reader Writer Streaming
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Command import-5 reads chunked JSON with an unknown field and a 2 MiB string, writes a report through a temporary path, crosses a time-zone boundary, and receives cancellation. Define streaming limits, atomicity, cleanup, stderr, and exit evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Reader Writer Streaming.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compose io.reader and io.writer boundaries, buffering, limited reads, copying, and streaming without assuming one read fills a buffer. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Reader Writer Streaming, keep Reader Writer Streaming connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
